shortDescription: The TCM has detected that the gear ratio in fourth gear does not match the expected value.
fullDescription: Code P0734 indicates the TCM has detected a gear ratio error in fourth gear. Fourth gear is typically the direct drive or overdrive gear used during highway cruising. A gear ratio error here means the transmission is slipping or not fully engaging fourth gear, which can result in higher RPM at cruising speed, reduced fuel economy, and increased transmission heat.
safetyImpact: Slipping in fourth gear during highway driving can cause overheating and loss of power. The transmission may downshift unexpectedly.

causes.cause: Worn fourth gear clutch pack or overdrive components
Valve body issue affecting fourth gear

symptoms: Higher RPM than normal at highway speeds

fixSteps.title: Check ATF
fixSteps.instruction: Verify transmission fluid level and condition. Low or burnt fluid is a primary cause of high-gear slipping.

fixSteps.title: Monitor gear ratios
fixSteps.instruction: Use a scan tool to monitor input/output speed ratios in fourth gear. Compare to manufacturer specifications.

fixSteps.title: Service the transmission
fixSteps.instruction: Change the ATF and filter. In some cases this resolves mild fourth gear slipping.

fixSteps.title: Rebuild or replace transmission
fixSteps.instruction: If the issue persists, the fourth gear or overdrive clutch pack requires replacement through a transmission overhaul.

faq.q: Will higher RPM at highway speed damage the engine?
faq.a: Not immediately, but it increases fuel consumption, engine wear, and transmission heat. The issue should be addressed.

faq.q: Can I just avoid using overdrive?
faq.a: While this prevents the slip, it is not a solution. The underlying problem will worsen and may affect other gears.

faq.q: What is the typical fourth gear ratio?
faq.a: It varies by transmission. In many 4-speed automatics, fourth gear is a 1:1 direct drive or an overdrive ratio around 0.70:1.

title: Torque Converter Clutch Circuit
shortDescription: The TCM/PCM has detected a malfunction in the torque converter clutch (TCC) solenoid circuit.
fullDescription: Code P0740 is set when the TCM or PCM detects an electrical fault in the torque converter clutch solenoid circuit. The TCC locks the torque converter at highway speeds to eliminate slip between the engine and transmission, improving fuel economy and reducing heat. When this circuit fails, the converter cannot lock up, resulting in higher RPM at cruise speeds, reduced fuel economy, and potential overheating.
safetyImpact: Generally safe to drive, but fuel economy suffers and the transmission may overheat during extended highway driving.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring in the TCC circuit
Dirty transmission fluid clogging the solenoid

symptoms: Higher RPM than normal at highway speeds
Slight shudder when converter should lock
Transmission overheating on long highway trips

fixSteps.title: Check for TCC operation
fixSteps.instruction: Use a scan tool to command TCC lockup. Monitor engine RPM for a slight drop (50-100 RPM) indicating lockup. No drop means the TCC is not engaging.

fixSteps.title: Test the TCC solenoid circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Check the TCC solenoid resistance at the transmission connector. Compare to specifications. Test for power and ground in the circuit.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wiring between the TCM/PCM and the transmission connector for damage, corrosion, or shorts.

fixSteps.title: Replace TCC solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: The TCC solenoid is typically located inside the transmission in the valve body. Replacing it requires dropping the transmission pan and accessing the valve body.

faq.q: What is torque converter lockup?
faq.a: The torque converter has an internal clutch that mechanically connects the engine to the transmission at highway speeds, eliminating slip and improving fuel economy.

faq.q: Can I drive without TCC lockup?
faq.a: Yes, but fuel economy will decrease by 2-5 MPG and the transmission may run hotter during extended highway driving.

faq.q: Where is the TCC solenoid?
faq.a: It is typically inside the transmission, mounted in or on the valve body. Accessing it requires removing the transmission pan.

title: Torque Converter Clutch Circuit Performance/Stuck Off
shortDescription: The TCM has detected that the torque converter clutch is not engaging when commanded, or its performance is outside acceptable parameters.
fullDescription: Code P0741 is set when the TCM commands the torque converter clutch to engage but the expected change in RPM slip does not occur. This means the TCC is stuck off or is not fully locking. Unlike P0740 which indicates a circuit fault, P0741 indicates a performance issue where the solenoid may be operating but the clutch is not functioning properly. This could be caused by a worn clutch, stuck solenoid, or hydraulic issues.
safetyImpact: Fuel economy decreases and the transmission may overheat during highway driving. Generally safe for normal driving.

causes.cause: TCC solenoid stuck in the off position
Valve body issue preventing TCC apply pressure
Internal seal leak reducing TCC apply pressure

symptoms: Higher engine RPM at highway speeds
No lockup feel at steady cruise

fixSteps.title: Change transmission fluid
fixSteps.instruction: A fluid change can sometimes resolve stuck solenoids and restore clutch friction. Use only the manufacturer-specified ATF.

fixSteps.title: Test TCC operation
fixSteps.instruction: Use a scan tool to monitor TCC slip at highway speeds. Normal slip should be near zero when locked. Significant slip confirms the problem.

fixSteps.title: Replace TCC solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Access the valve body by removing the transmission pan. Replace the TCC solenoid and inspect the valve body for wear.

fixSteps.title: Replace torque converter
fixSteps.instruction: If the TCC clutch material is worn, the torque converter must be replaced. This requires removing the transmission from the vehicle.

faq.q: What is the difference between P0740 and P0741?
faq.a: P0740 indicates an electrical fault in the TCC circuit. P0741 indicates the solenoid may work electrically but the clutch is not physically engaging properly.

faq.q: Can a fluid change fix P0741?
faq.a: Sometimes. If the solenoid is sticking due to contamination, fresh fluid can help. If the clutch material is worn, a fluid change will not fix it.

faq.q: What is TCC slip?
faq.a: TCC slip is the RPM difference between the engine and the transmission input shaft. When the TCC is locked, slip should be near zero.

title: Torque Converter Clutch Circuit Electrical
shortDescription: The TCM/PCM has detected a specific electrical fault in the torque converter clutch solenoid circuit.
fullDescription: Code P0743 is set when the TCM or PCM detects an electrical problem specifically in the TCC solenoid circuit, such as an open, short to ground, or short to power. This is a more specific electrical fault code than P0740 and indicates a definitive wiring or solenoid failure. The TCC solenoid cannot be controlled properly, preventing torque converter lockup.
safetyImpact: TCC lockup will not function. Fuel economy decreases and transmission may overheat during extended highway use.

causes.cause: Internal short or open in the TCC solenoid
Damaged wiring in the internal transmission harness
External wiring damage between TCM and transmission

symptoms: Higher cruise RPM and reduced fuel economy
Shudder or vibration at highway speeds

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the TCC solenoid resistance at the transmission connector. Typical values are 10-25 ohms. Open or shorted readings indicate solenoid failure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect transmission connector
fixSteps.instruction: Check the external transmission connector for corrosion, water intrusion, or damaged pins. This is a common failure point.

fixSteps.title: Test wiring to TCM
fixSteps.instruction: Check continuity of the TCC solenoid wires from the transmission connector to the TCM. Look for shorts and opens.

fixSteps.title: Replace TCC solenoid or harness
fixSteps.instruction: Drop the transmission pan and replace the TCC solenoid. If the internal harness is damaged, it must also be replaced.

faq.q: What makes P0743 different from P0740?
faq.a: P0743 specifically indicates an electrical fault in the solenoid or wiring, while P0740 is a more general TCC circuit malfunction code.

faq.q: Can I test the solenoid without dropping the pan?
faq.a: You can test the circuit resistance at the external transmission connector, but accessing the solenoid for replacement requires removing the pan.

faq.q: What damages the internal transmission harness?
faq.a: Heat, ATF degradation, and age can break down the insulation on internal harness wires, causing shorts or opens.

title: Torque Converter Clutch Circuit Intermittent
shortDescription: The TCM has detected intermittent operation of the torque converter clutch circuit.
fullDescription: Code P0744 indicates the TCM has detected that the TCC circuit is operating intermittently. The TCC may lock and unlock erratically, or the electrical signal to the solenoid is inconsistent. This can cause a shudder or hunting sensation during highway driving as the converter repeatedly locks and unlocks. Intermittent faults are often caused by loose connections, degraded wiring, or a solenoid that is beginning to fail.
safetyImpact: The vehicle may exhibit shuddering at highway speeds. The condition is annoying but generally not immediately dangerous.

causes.cause: Intermittent connection in the TCC solenoid circuit

symptoms: TCC lockup hunting (repeatedly locking and unlocking)

fixSteps.title: Check connector and wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the transmission external connector for looseness, corrosion, or water intrusion. Wiggle the connector while monitoring TCC operation.

fixSteps.title: Change transmission fluid
fixSteps.instruction: Contaminated fluid can cause solenoid sticking. A fluid and filter change may resolve intermittent TCC issues.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid and circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Measure TCC solenoid resistance and check for intermittent readings by gently tapping the solenoid or wiggling wires. Any fluctuation indicates a fault.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid or harness
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the TCC solenoid. If the internal harness shows damage, replace it as well. Verify consistent TCC operation after repair.

faq.q: Why does the car shudder at highway speed?
faq.a: The TCC is repeatedly locking and unlocking, causing the engine and transmission to alternately couple and decouple. This creates a shuddering sensation.

faq.q: Are intermittent codes harder to diagnose?
faq.a: Yes. Intermittent faults can be challenging because the problem may not be present during testing. Wiggle tests and monitoring over time can help.

faq.q: Can I just ignore P0744?
faq.a: It is not recommended. The intermittent fault will likely worsen, and the repeated locking/unlocking generates excessive heat in the converter.

title: Pressure Control Solenoid 'A'
shortDescription: The TCM has detected a malfunction in the transmission pressure control solenoid A circuit.
fullDescription: Code P0745 is set when the TCM detects an electrical fault in the transmission line pressure control solenoid A. This solenoid regulates the hydraulic pressure within the transmission, which is critical for smooth and firm gear engagement. If the solenoid fails, the transmission may have excessively harsh or soft shifts, slipping, or complete loss of gear engagement. Line pressure control is fundamental to automatic transmission operation.
safetyImpact: Transmission may shift very harshly or slip severely. The vehicle may enter limp mode or lose gear engagement entirely.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring in the solenoid circuit
Dirty ATF causing solenoid to stick

fixSteps.title: Check ATF condition
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect transmission fluid level and quality. Contaminated fluid is a leading cause of solenoid failures.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid electrically
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the pressure control solenoid resistance at the transmission connector. Compare to manufacturer specifications.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Test the wiring between the TCM and transmission connector for continuity, shorts to ground, and shorts to power.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the transmission pan and replace the pressure control solenoid. Change the fluid and filter at the same time.

faq.q: What does the pressure control solenoid do?
faq.a: It regulates the hydraulic line pressure in the transmission. Proper line pressure ensures clutch packs and bands engage firmly but smoothly.

faq.q: Can I drive with harsh shifting?
faq.a: Briefly. Excessively harsh shifting can damage internal transmission components. Have it diagnosed promptly.

faq.q: How often should transmission fluid be changed?
faq.a: Every 30,000-60,000 miles for most vehicles. Regular changes help prevent solenoid contamination and extend transmission life.

title: Shift Solenoid 'A'
shortDescription: The TCM has detected a malfunction in transmission shift solenoid A.
fullDescription: Code P0750 is set when the TCM detects an electrical fault in shift solenoid A. Shift solenoids control the flow of hydraulic fluid to engage different gear combinations within the automatic transmission. Solenoid A is typically involved in controlling first and second gear operation. A failure of this solenoid can prevent proper gear engagement and cause the transmission to operate in a limited number of gears.
safetyImpact: The transmission may not shift properly, potentially staying in one gear. This limits speed and acceleration capability.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring in the solenoid circuit

fixSteps.title: Scan for codes
fixSteps.instruction: Read all TCM codes. Multiple solenoid codes may indicate a common wiring or fluid issue rather than individual solenoid failures.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure shift solenoid A resistance at the transmission connector. Typical range is 12-25 ohms depending on the application.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring and connector
fixSteps.instruction: Check the transmission external connector and wiring for damage. Test the solenoid feed wire from the TCM for continuity and shorts.

fixSteps.title: Replace shift solenoid A
fixSteps.instruction: Drop the transmission pan, remove the old solenoid from the valve body, and install a new one. Replace the fluid and filter.

faq.q: Which gears does shift solenoid A control?
faq.a: This varies by transmission model. Solenoid A typically controls first and second gear combinations, but check your specific service manual.

faq.q: Can I replace just one solenoid?
faq.a: Yes, but many technicians recommend replacing the entire solenoid pack since the other solenoids are likely the same age and may fail soon.

faq.q: Is a solenoid pack expensive?
faq.a: A complete solenoid pack ranges from $50-$300 depending on the transmission. Individual solenoids are $20-$80.

title: Shift Solenoid 'A' Performance/Stuck Off
shortDescription: The TCM has detected that shift solenoid A is not performing correctly or is stuck in the off position.
fullDescription: Code P0751 indicates the TCM has commanded shift solenoid A to engage but has not observed the expected gear change. The solenoid may be physically stuck due to contamination, worn, or mechanically jammed. Unlike P0750 which is an electrical fault, P0751 is a performance code indicating the solenoid is not achieving the desired result even though it may be receiving the correct electrical signal.
safetyImpact: The transmission may not shift into certain gears, causing limp mode or limited gear operation.

causes.cause: Shift solenoid A stuck due to contamination
Valve body wear preventing proper fluid routing

symptoms: Failure to shift into certain gears

fixSteps.title: Change transmission fluid
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the ATF and filter. Contaminated fluid is the most common cause of stuck solenoids. Use only manufacturer-specified fluid.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid function
fixSteps.instruction: Use a scan tool with bidirectional control to command the solenoid on and off while monitoring for the expected gear change.

fixSteps.title: Replace shift solenoid A
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the solenoid from the valve body. Inspect it for contamination and debris. Install a new solenoid.

fixSteps.title: Inspect valve body
fixSteps.instruction: If replacing the solenoid does not resolve the issue, the valve body may have worn bores or stuck valves that require reconditioning or replacement.

faq.q: What does 'stuck off' mean?
faq.a: It means the solenoid is not moving to its commanded on position, so it remains in its default off state.

faq.q: Can a fluid change fix a stuck solenoid?
faq.a: Often yes. If the solenoid is stuck due to varnish or debris, fresh fluid can dissolve the contamination and restore operation.

faq.q: What is the difference between P0750 and P0751?
faq.a: P0750 is an electrical fault code (open/short). P0751 is a performance code meaning the solenoid is receiving the signal but not performing correctly.

title: Shift Solenoid 'B'
shortDescription: The TCM has detected an electrical malfunction in transmission shift solenoid B.
fullDescription: Code P0755 is set when the TCM detects an electrical fault in shift solenoid B. This solenoid, in combination with solenoid A, controls gear selection in the automatic transmission. Solenoid B is typically involved in third and fourth gear engagement. An electrical failure prevents the TCM from controlling this solenoid, resulting in limited gear availability and potentially harsh shifting.
safetyImpact: Transmission may not shift into higher gears, forcing the engine to run at higher RPM and limiting vehicle speed capability.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring in the solenoid B circuit

fixSteps.title: Read all codes
fixSteps.instruction: Check for multiple solenoid codes. Multiple codes may indicate a common wiring or connector issue.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure shift solenoid B resistance at the transmission connector. An out-of-range reading confirms solenoid failure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check the external and internal transmission wiring for damage. Test continuity from the TCM to the solenoid.

fixSteps.title: Replace shift solenoid B
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the transmission pan and replace solenoid B on the valve body. Service the fluid and filter.

faq.q: Which gears does shift solenoid B affect?
faq.a: Solenoid B typically controls third and fourth gear engagement, though this varies by transmission model.

faq.q: Should I replace all solenoids at once?
faq.a: It is recommended since the other solenoids are the same age. A solenoid pack replacement is often more economical long-term.

faq.q: Can I test the solenoid without removing it?
faq.a: You can test resistance at the external transmission connector. Functional testing requires a scan tool with bidirectional control.

title: Shift Solenoid 'B' Performance/Stuck Off
shortDescription: The TCM has detected that shift solenoid B is not performing correctly or is stuck in the off position.
fullDescription: Code P0756 indicates the TCM has commanded shift solenoid B to engage but the expected gear change has not occurred. The solenoid is receiving the electrical command but is not mechanically performing. This is usually caused by contamination, wear, or hydraulic issues in the valve body. The transmission may be stuck in a limited range of gears.
safetyImpact: Limited gear availability affects acceleration and top speed. Highway driving may not be possible if upper gears are unavailable.

causes.cause: Shift solenoid B stuck due to contamination

symptoms: Unable to shift into higher gears

fixSteps.title: Change ATF
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the fluid and filter with manufacturer-specified ATF. This alone may free a contaminated solenoid.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid response
fixSteps.instruction: Use a scan tool to command solenoid B while monitoring transmission behavior. Compare expected vs actual gear engagement.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid B
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and replace the solenoid. Inspect the old solenoid for contamination and debris.

fixSteps.title: Service valve body if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If the new solenoid does not resolve the issue, the valve body may need reconditioning or replacement.

faq.q: Is P0756 the same as P0755?
faq.a: No. P0755 is an electrical fault. P0756 is a performance fault where the solenoid has power but is not mechanically working.

faq.q: What contamination causes solenoids to stick?
faq.a: Varnish from degraded ATF, metal particles from clutch and gear wear, and debris from seals breaking down can all clog solenoid passages.

faq.q: Can I drive without higher gears?
faq.a: Only at low speeds. Without upper gears, the engine will rev excessively at any significant speed, causing overheating and excess wear.

title: Shift Solenoid 'C'
shortDescription: The TCM has detected an electrical malfunction in transmission shift solenoid C.
fullDescription: Code P0760 is set when the TCM detects an electrical fault in shift solenoid C. In transmissions with more than four speeds, additional solenoids like solenoid C are used to control higher gear combinations including fifth and sixth gears or overdrive functions. An electrical failure in this solenoid limits the transmission to fewer gears and may trigger limp mode.
safetyImpact: Higher gears may be unavailable, limiting highway speed capability and fuel economy.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring in the solenoid C circuit

symptoms: No overdrive or higher gear engagement

fixSteps.title: Scan and diagnose
fixSteps.instruction: Read all TCM codes. Check if multiple solenoid codes are present, indicating a possible common cause.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid electrically
fixSteps.instruction: Measure solenoid C resistance at the transmission connector. Compare to specifications.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring and harness
fixSteps.instruction: Test the circuit from the TCM to the solenoid for continuity and shorts. Check internal and external harness condition.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid C
fixSteps.instruction: Access the valve body and replace solenoid C. Service the fluid and filter simultaneously.

faq.q: What does solenoid C control?
faq.a: In multi-speed transmissions (5-speed, 6-speed), solenoid C typically controls the higher gear combinations or overdrive function.

faq.q: Is my transmission a 5-speed or 6-speed?
faq.a: Check your owner's manual or vehicle specifications. The number of shift solenoids corresponds to the complexity of the transmission.

faq.q: Can I drive safely without overdrive?
faq.a: Yes, at lower speeds. Highway driving will result in higher RPM and increased fuel consumption.

title: Shift Solenoid 'C' Performance/Stuck Off
shortDescription: The TCM has detected that shift solenoid C is not performing correctly or is stuck in the off position.
fullDescription: Code P0761 indicates the TCM has commanded shift solenoid C to engage but the expected shift has not occurred. The solenoid may be receiving the electrical signal but is mechanically stuck or unable to move the internal valve. This prevents the transmission from accessing all available gears, typically affecting overdrive or higher gear engagement.
safetyImpact: Limited gear availability. The vehicle can be driven at moderate speeds but highway driving efficiency is compromised.

causes.cause: Shift solenoid C stuck due to contamination
Valve body wear or stuck valve

symptoms: Cannot access overdrive or higher gears
Higher engine RPM at highway speeds

fixSteps.title: Service transmission fluid
fixSteps.instruction: Drain and replace the ATF and filter. Fresh fluid may free a stuck solenoid.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid performance
fixSteps.instruction: Use bidirectional scan tool controls to command solenoid C. Monitor for expected gear changes.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and replace solenoid C from the valve body. Inspect the old solenoid and surrounding area for debris.

fixSteps.title: Valve body inspection
fixSteps.instruction: If a new solenoid does not fix the issue, inspect the valve body for worn bores or stuck check balls.

faq.q: How do I know which gear solenoid C controls?
faq.a: The solenoid-to-gear mapping varies by transmission model. Check your specific transmission service manual for the solenoid application chart.

faq.q: Should I replace all solenoids when one fails?
faq.a: It is recommended. If one solenoid has failed due to age and contamination, the others are likely close to failure as well.

faq.q: Can I buy a solenoid pack instead of individual solenoids?
faq.a: Yes. Many suppliers sell complete solenoid packs which include all shift and pressure solenoids. This is often more economical.

title: Shift Solenoid 'D'
shortDescription: The TCM has detected an electrical malfunction in transmission shift solenoid D.
fullDescription: Code P0765 is set when the TCM detects an electrical fault in shift solenoid D. This solenoid is found in transmissions with five or more speeds and is used in combination with other shift solenoids to control the higher gear combinations. Solenoid D may control specific overdrive ratios or assist in smooth transitions between gears. An electrical failure limits gear availability.
safetyImpact: Certain gears may be unavailable. The transmission may enter limp mode, limiting vehicle performance.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring in the solenoid D circuit

fixSteps.title: Read all transmission codes
fixSteps.instruction: Scan for all TCM codes. Determine if solenoid D is the only failure or if other solenoids are also affected.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid D resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the solenoid resistance at the transmission connector. An open or shorted reading confirms a solenoid or wiring failure.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring integrity
fixSteps.instruction: Test the wiring from the TCM to the transmission connector and the internal harness to the solenoid.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid D
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the transmission pan and replace solenoid D. Consider replacing the entire solenoid pack. Service the fluid and filter.

faq.q: Do all transmissions have a solenoid D?
faq.a: No. Solenoid D is typically found in transmissions with 5 or more speeds. 4-speed transmissions usually only have solenoids A and B.

faq.q: What happens if solenoid D fails?
faq.a: The transmission loses the ability to engage certain gear combinations, often resulting in limp mode with a limited number of available gears.

faq.q: Can I test the solenoid without removing the pan?
faq.a: You can test electrical resistance at the external connector, but physical inspection and replacement require pan removal.

title: Shift Solenoid 'E'
shortDescription: The TCM has detected an electrical malfunction in transmission shift solenoid E.
fullDescription: Code P0770 is set when the TCM detects an electrical fault in shift solenoid E. Found in advanced transmissions with six or more speeds, solenoid E works with the other shift solenoids to provide the wide range of gear ratios needed for optimal performance and fuel economy. A failure of solenoid E reduces the available gear combinations and may cause the TCM to enter a fail-safe mode.
safetyImpact: Reduced gear availability may limit vehicle performance. Limp mode operation restricts speed and acceleration.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring in the solenoid E circuit

symptoms: Missing gears in the shift pattern

fixSteps.title: Comprehensive code scan
fixSteps.instruction: Read all TCM codes. In multi-speed transmissions, multiple solenoid codes often point to common causes.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid E electrically
fixSteps.instruction: Measure solenoid E resistance at the transmission connector. Compare to specifications.

fixSteps.title: Inspect all wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check internal and external transmission harness wiring for damage, corrosion, or shorts.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid E or solenoid pack
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the transmission pan and valve body cover. Replace solenoid E or the entire solenoid pack. Service the fluid and filter.

faq.q: What transmission has solenoid E?
faq.a: Typically 6-speed and higher automatic transmissions. Consult your service manual to determine your transmission's solenoid configuration.

faq.q: Are all shift solenoids the same?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Some transmissions use solenoids of different sizes, types (on/off vs. PWM), and resistance values for different functions.

faq.q: How long do transmission solenoids last?
faq.a: With regular fluid maintenance, solenoids can last 150,000+ miles. Neglected fluid changes significantly shorten their lifespan.

title: Pressure Control Solenoid 'B'
shortDescription: The TCM has detected an electrical malfunction in transmission pressure control solenoid B.
fullDescription: Code P0775 is set when the TCM detects an electrical fault in pressure control solenoid B. This solenoid works in conjunction with pressure control solenoid A to regulate hydraulic pressure in the transmission. In some transmissions, solenoid B controls pressure for specific gear ranges or provides supplemental pressure control. A failure can result in harsh shifting, slipping, or the transmission entering a protective fail-safe mode with maximum line pressure.
safetyImpact: The transmission may shift very harshly (if pressure defaults high) or slip (if pressure is too low). Either condition can cause secondary damage if not addressed.

causes.cause: Open or shorted wiring in the solenoid circuit
Contaminated transmission fluid causing solenoid sticking

fixSteps.title: Check transmission fluid
fixSteps.instruction: Verify fluid level and condition. Degraded fluid is a primary contributor to pressure solenoid failures.

fixSteps.title: Test solenoid B
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the resistance of pressure control solenoid B at the transmission connector. Values outside the specified range indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Check circuit wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Test the wiring between the TCM and the solenoid for continuity, shorts to ground, and shorts to power.

fixSteps.title: Replace solenoid and service transmission
fixSteps.instruction: Drop the pan, replace pressure control solenoid B (or the entire solenoid pack), replace the filter, and refill with fresh ATF.

faq.q: What is the difference between a shift solenoid and a pressure control solenoid?
faq.a: Shift solenoids direct fluid to engage specific gears. Pressure control solenoids regulate the overall hydraulic pressure used to apply clutches and bands.

faq.q: Can high line pressure damage the transmission?
faq.a: Yes. Sustained high pressure causes harsh shifts that can damage clutch packs, bands, and other internal components.

faq.q: Should I replace both pressure solenoids at the same time?
faq.a: Yes, it is recommended to replace both A and B pressure solenoids together since they experience similar wear and contamination.
